title: Trump Is Finally Eyeing an Exit From Iran. But Will He Take It?
summary: President Trump says he is considering “winding down” operations in Iran. But many of his original war goals remain unaccomplished.

title: Gas Prices Have Risen More Than 30% in Some States in Weeks Since Iran War Began
summary: Across the South and Southwest, where price hikes have been the most severe, drivers have lamented how the increased costs have cut into their budgets.

title: Israel Orders Military to Intensify Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
summary: Israel Katz, the defense minister, said he ordered troops to destroy more bridges and buildings in southern Lebanon, stoking worries that Israel was widening a military-controlled buffer zone there.

title: ICE to Help TSA at Airports Amid Partial Shutdown, Trump’s Border Czar Says
summary: Tom Homan, President Trump’s chief border official, cast the operation largely as one to help ease long security lines that have been frustrating passengers at U.S. airports.

title: Mullin Explored Bipartisan Deal to Rein in Immigration Crackdown
summary: The G.O.P. senator President Trump chose to lead the Department of Homeland Security privately discussed concessions the White House has repeatedly rejected.

title: News Outlets Pressure Pentagon to Restore Access After Court Ruling
summary: A federal judge ruled on Friday that key parts of the Pentagon’s press policy were unconstitutional.

title: The Supreme Court Could Make it Harder to Vote by Mail in the Midterms
summary: The Republican National Committee wants to toss ballots arriving after Election Day. Critics say thousands of votes — a majority cast by Democrats — are at stake.

title: How the Supreme Court Could Change Voting by Mail
summary: The Supreme Court is ruling on whether ballots received after Election Day should be disqualified. Our national politics reporter Nick Corasaniti explains how a Supreme Court ruling could affect hundreds of thousands of Americans in rural and urban areas.

title: Beshear Criticizes Vance in Ohio, Stoking 2028 Presidential Tensions
summary: Mr. Beshear, the Democratic governor of Kentucky, sharply criticized Mr. Vance as both angle for potential presidential runs.

title: California Could Elect a Republican Governor Thanks to the ‘Jungle Primary’
summary: The state’s wide-open “jungle primary” has Democrats on edge.

title: Don Lemon on Trump, Minnesota and Streaming on YouTube
summary: A Minnesota church protest made the former anchor a star again — and a target for the Trump administration.

title: The Billionaire Funding France’s Far Right
summary: Pierre-Édouard Stérin is financing projects to make France less Muslim, more Catholic and more capitalist. He says his program has trained thousands running for municipal office on Sunday.

title: France Is Voting for Thousands of New Mayors. Here’s Why It Matters.
summary: The far-right party had first-round successes in several cities, but so did the far left. Sunday’s second round may clarify who has real momentum.

title: Trump Has Made a Fundamental Miscalculation about Iran
summary: America shouldn’t fight a war like this.

title: The End of the Free-Range ‘Stand by Me’ Childhood
summary: What a 40-year-old movie told me about childhood today.

title: Introducing the Robot Umpire
summary: We explain a technological change that’s about to transform baseball.

title: They Want to Stop Paying Taxes as a Protest. There Are Consequences.
summary: Some tax resisters withhold a symbolic amount, but those who willfully refuse to file or pay could face civil or criminal penalties.

title: Italy’s Leader Wants to Change the Constitution. Italians Don’t Get It.
summary: Voting starts Sunday on a referendum to overhaul Italy’s judiciary. Many people don’t understand the complex proposal — and it could hurt Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.

title: Not All Malls Are Struggling
summary: A certain type of shopping mall has become a surprising bright spot for real estate investors.
